product_title: Echo Show 8 (2nd Gen, 2021 release) - Glacier White - with 30-day FREE trial of Alexa Together (monthly auto-renewal)
combined_reviews:
Review 1 Rating: 1/5 Title: Big Disappointment- Gen 2 Echo8 is a Nightmare! Text: This was purchased to replace an Echo 5 that I loved, but had been bricked after the latest over -the- air update from Amazon. It was ruined by the update and never recovered. This one is not anything like my original. There is a giant digital clock that I cannot change… the old echo five had a setting for this- the background adjustment control has also disappeared! Now it is called clock and photo display- and there is NO setting for background! I already had a photo for my background in my Alexa app and added it to the new Echo 8- but it does not show as a background choice now, just part of a rotating photo display, which in my case I do not want. I am unable to get the background to stay on instead it disappears and the only thing showing is the giant clock numbers which I do NOT want to see! I tried every setting but as I said the settings in new version removed any background and clock setting as was available in the old Alexa app and on screen settings. There are constant rotations of things even though I have turned off everything or told this new Echo to hide them- I just want the screen to show my background photo and the little clock in the top left corner like my old echo5 did. The new one insists on showing me recipes ( I Don’t cook) or a song that I played weeks and weeks ago and don’t want to see or hear about UNTIL I ask the Echo for it. Finally, I had to unplug this thing last night as the screen would not and I repeat, would not ever remove that darn clock! It is pretty big and there is no setting to make it smaller or dimmer- the new low power mode ( which I also do not want) dims the screen, removes my backgound picture ( which I want left up) and lo and behold, what is left glaringly bright on the screen - that digital clock in bright white big numbers! If I wanted a digital clock I would have purchased one. Asking this Echo to do the things I used to ask on my old little show5 is not intuitive at all and instead made slower and more difficult. I was able to find a background setting on an older ipad which had a previous version of the alexa app- Just an FYI, I used that to add my background pix and it still would not stay on the screen of this new model- and as I said, the old settings on both Alexa app and the Echo show itself have been changed as have some other things such as how often the rotation of things you do not want to see appear on the screen. Telling this echo to hide them or removing them in the app had little affect. So I am likely sending it back after I deregister etc.- very, very disappointed… and was quite content before the over the air update broke my original echo show- “sometimes no change is better” is a hint I’ll give to the young developers behind the scenes…
*** In all fairness, Amazon called today after this review went live and did indicate they would pass along my concerns and problems using the new software and settings on this model- appreciated the effort and if I do keep this new device or get another- perhaps an update will come giving users a choice on background and clock as well as the low power mode- choices on settings empower users to control the devices they bought and own… Review 2 Rating: 1/5 Title: Echo Show 5 is better Text: I had an Echo Show 5 (2nd gen) in the kitchen in front of the sink in a bay window so I replaced it with this Echo Show 8.
The first thing I noticed is how heavy it was. It's way too heavy for what it is.
Second dislike was the sound. It's not that much louder than the Echo Show 5 and lacks a lot of highs. Good enough for news but they just have to add some small tweeters to the sides to make the audio a lot clearer. My 3watt $10 computer speakers sound clearer. Voices and bass is good enough but don't expect clear highs from it.
Third dislike is the connection to my firetv recast. The Echo Show 5 I could say "show channel guide" and it would give me a list of shows on now. The Echo Show 8 says I have 2 fire TV recasts and to configure it in the app. The app only shows one fire tv recast. The Echo Show 5 I setup in my office still lets me show channel guide and it will show what's on. I can still watch live tv but I have to know the station name. I can't say "Alexa, tune to CW" or "Alexa, tune to NBC". I have to say "Alexa, tune to the CW" or Alexa, tune to the NBC".
The camera is good when it works. I have to wait 10 seconds before the video shows up when dropping in on it. I think it's doing it's face tracking when the camera starts. The image looks like 64 little scenes at start up and then it eventually goes down to one.

I thought the Echo Show 8 was supposed to function a lot better than a 2nd Gen Echo Show 5 but it doesn't. It's just bigger, heavier, and has a better camera. I didn't expect to have problems working with my Fire TV recast, poor sound, or it being so heavy. Reviews I read before buying didn't mention these issues. Review 3 Rating: 1/5 Title: This is one of the worst purchases I've ever made Text: Update April 10th: Amazon reached out to me after I made this review and offered to exchange my old Echo Show with a replacement. It worked great for a few days and then the same issues started again (runtime errors). In fact, it's happening more often with the new device they sent me. Basically I have two bricks now and I have to go out of my way to return the first brick while I still have issues with the second brick they sent me. This is beyond ridiculous. Also, Amazon verifies when you are having issues with your device (they can see how many times you receive errors on your devices and how many times you have to do a factory reset) so they are well aware of what is happening to me.

I will try to explain how horrible this item is. I purchased two Echo Shows five months ago and used the monthly payments which was fine (in hindsight if I had to pay for both upfront I never would have purchased them). I have had issues with these devices from day one.

First of all, Echo Show doesn't come with a remote which of course I knew beforehand but I didn't think it was an issue. After all, you just turn on your favorite shows or movies and the autoplay so why would you even need a remote right? Wrong. These devices continously have "runtime" errors, connection errors and playback errors and when this happens your shows and movies stop every time.

So what do you have to do? That's right, scream at Alexa to restart or get up and manually start the program again like it's the 1970s. Where is the convenience in that? The worst part is it used to come with a remote and apparently Amazon thought convenience wasn't important to their customers and discontinued the add-on.

Whenever I contact "support" about these runtime, connection and playback errors everyone chats or speaks to me like I'm speaking an alienic language. The ONLY thing that works is resetting the entire device which means you have to start everything over including your wifi and account login. Who has time for all of that? This has happened to me over 10 times in the last week. Keep in mind this device is only five months old! What I thought was interesting was these problems really ramped up right after I made my final payment and now I'm stuck with this thing.

As far as adding other apps or skills like Hulu, good luck. You can't just pull up a list of skills without scanning the internet for a minimum of 15 minutes trying to figure out how to add it to your device. I think it started working when I enabled the Hulu skill on my Alexa app (don't get me started on that app and the way it lags and just stops working at times) but don't quote me on that because it was almost like a fever dream that I've blocked out; that's how frustrating the process was.

Speaking of Hulu, as I said there are so many errors with Echo Show and Hulu it's ridiculous. Hulu works fine on other devices including Amazon's Fire Tablet but they are at war when the Show is involved. Amazon Prime video works but how many times can you watch Hanna before you're bored out of your mind?

Alexa is pretty decent and understands what you're saying most of the time but you have to be right next to the screen. The sound is also really good and I do appreciate that.

If I could take it back I would. It's a headache and so annoying when it just shuts off sporadically. Next time I'll just stop being cheap and buy a smart TV. At least it comes with a remote.
summary: Reviewer says his original Echo 5 was ruined by an over-the-air update. The new Echo 8 has a giant digital clock that he can't change. The app only shows one fire tv channel and it will show what's on live TV.

Review 1 Rating: 3/5 Title: Honestly a bit disappointed. I was expecting more Text: I sort of purchased this on a whim to see if I could fit it into my life somehow. I found the idea of a smart display intriguing as I already have 2 Fire Cubes and absolutely love them. However a Fire cube this is not. It's difficult to express but this thing is cumbersome to use and it's slow. For example. I was eating dinner and watching a video I purchased on Prime video. I had to pause it to get up and do something so I said "echo pause" Wich it did. When I returned the screen had defaulted back to the time home screen. No big deal right? So I said "echo play" and instead of resuming my show, it resumed a song I had paused earlier that day. I asked it to stop and then asked for it to play my show. It began to play a different episode and not where I left off. Getting it back to where I left off required more awkward voice control and touching the screen. Very annoying when I'm trying to eat my meal. This device also has trouble understanding my queries. It's getting my words incorrect. One feature I was pleasently surprised by was the sound quality. It is very good. The low end is impressive. Almost as good as my Nest Audio. Maybe with some software updates this device Will be good. But for now I'm still trying to understand it's intended flow. Review 2 Rating: 3/5 Title: Good but can improve a lot Text: I just received this and was really excited for it. I bought this for my room because my roommate often stays up when he gets home to watch his shows, but I refuse to get a TV for my room. Using the other Alexa parts was just a plus for me, honestly.

Positives for me are the sound quality (amazing), voice recognition is better than on my old echo dot, and beautiful display.

Things that I hope Amazon listens to and improves…
- This is sort of difficult to navigate. I’m really good with technology but oh my god I can’t imagine my mother trying to work this thing. I’m okay with it, but it’s irritatingly annoying to use.
- Lots of streaming services are wonky, even the ones that are Amazon based (twitch..????)
- It’s difficult to set certain things up, like connecting my Spotify took a hot minute

Otherwise, I really enjoy it and I hope that the next update Amazon puts out for it helps with some of the navigation and streaming. Review 3 Rating: 3/5 Title: Video quality and sound quality are great. Text: Only thing I don't like is when your watching a movie or something on it and you have to leave it paused for longer than a few minutes, it takes you all the way out of the video and you have to find it all over again.
summary: Reviewers were disappointed with the device. The sound quality is very good, almost as good as my Nest Audio. The device is cumbersome to use and is slow.

combined_reviews:
Review 1 Rating: 5/5 Title: A MUST HAVE! Text: [[VIDEOID:b6537fe65ba9d68258a80970448c9f4a]] Gah! I love how big this screen is!!!! I've been suffering downstairs without an Alexa in my new house and decided decided to Gift myself a housewarming and so glad I did! My son is also loving watching cartoons during breakfast 😏. This speaker is SUPER oud. Definitely all you would need to throw a house party AND have the neighbors knocking 😂. It's chic and pretty! Review 2 Rating: 5/5 Title: A good system for what it does. Text: Amazon Echo Show 8 review:
Me, it's not what I thought it would be.
Also me, it's not a bad device.
Also, also me, It's not what you'd think.
You can remotely login and watch areas it's in, watch multiple cameras that are connected all over the place, look up content, listen to music (I'm listening to EYE OF THE TIGER) and it pretty much is a KARAOKE system, among other things.
In short, it's a smart speaker (yes, a "SMART SPEAKER"). You can't easily or at all, install third-party applications on it, requires the Alexa App, but if you want to control other Amazon/Alexa devices and such that are compatible, it's not a bad device.
I can control my TV (I will not explain that, too much of a pain in the ass), lights, and other items just from it. It's a great "hub", or a damn good device. For now, it's on sale, and for AP ("Amazon Prime") members, there's a discount.
For recipes, watching videos, among other things, it's a good device. Would I go against buying it? Depends what you're expecting from it, otherwise, it's not a bad investment -- and a good gift, especially for the elderly that want something simple.
I've been using mine for the following:
1. Tracking weather
2. Managing devices (near and far)
3. Monitoring my room when I am not home. (Alexa app required)
4. Listening to music (The sound is great for those that don't mind the best, or care for the best, etc., and better than low-end IMHO).
5. Watching movies (Halloween Ends, GOOD!) But I switched to my T V, because I ended up having to lay down.
6. It DOES NOT COME WITH A BATTERY (extra item sold seprately) and a STAND that is a separate purchase, but does well on it's own otherwise.
If I were to rate, I'd give it a 7 out of 10

I posted this on my personal Facebook, it's a good product. Review 3 Rating: 5/5 Title: From sceptical to IMPRESSED Text: At first I was a bit apprehensive based on feedback from others and watching several YouTube videos about blah blah blah. So i took the plunge and bought one of these just to test it out and see how it performs as I wanted my own experience to be the judge, and boy oh boy, i am officially impressed.

SOUND
The sound is clean and you would not believe the high volume that is coming out of this device. I have mine set to about a little over quarter and it is just good for everything on a daily basis.

SCREEN
I think the 8 inch screen hits the sweet spot, not too big, not too small. Its clear, the touch response is good so far with no complaints.

ALEXA
Damn, that woman can catch her name every time, all the time. She doesn't miss a beat and she probably misunderstood what we asked her to do but after the basics she is quite capable of executing skills.

MY USAGE
I have this device connected to my home security cameras (LOREX) and the integration is very good. I can ask Alexa to view different cameras and I'm not disappointed by how she handles requests.
The "Drop In" feature is nice as sometimes I check in with my family when I'm not at home.
YouTube is functional, in that you have to ask Alexa to open YouTube and then you manually input your choices, Netflix goes through a similar process, but all in all it works and its functional.

NOTE
I liked it so much that i bought another one for the Kitchen.

NOTE 2
I found it so useful that i bought 2 for my in-laws to also view their security cameras and YouTube.

CONS
(1) I'm not a big fan of the split screen keyboard... probably there is a way to adjust it and I probably haven't figured it out as yet.
(2) Sometimes the cameras open on screen like a picture in picture and i have to pinch to zoom. It does this sometimes and again its probably a setting i have to adjust.

Overall i think this is a very good device based on your use-case and it works well for me.
summary: The Amazon Echo Show is a smart speaker with an 8-inch screen. Users can remotely login and watch areas it's in, watch multiple cameras that are connected all over the place, look up content, listen to music and more.

product_title: Soundcore Anker Life P2 True Wireless Earbuds, Clear Sound, USB C, 40H Playtime, IPX7 Waterproof, Wireless Earphones for Work, Home Office
combined_reviews:
Review 1 Rating: 1/5 Title: Quality is just not there Text: ***UPDATE*** 6/8/20
After posting my original review Anker reached out to me in an effort to make it right and make sure I was satisfied which I appreciated. Most companies wouldn’t even acknowledge a customers dissatisfaction much less try to improve their experience. Their customer service is A+ for sure! They offered to send me an upgraded version of their earbuds to try out the Liberty Air 2. They shipped them out immediately and arrived in just a few days. The difference in the Liberty Air 2 is incredible! I have not lost connection, they sound great for music and phone calls, no one has complained they can’t hear me and they have not fallen out of my ear at all. I am so happy with this set of earbuds that I just purchased a set for my Dad for Father’s Day. If you aren’t happy with the Anker Soundcore Life P2 or any other Bluetooth earbuds you’ve tried I highly recommend you the Liberty Air 2. You will not be disappointed! I have tried $250 earbuds that don’t compare to these.

I use earbuds for calls only and really wanted to love these but unfortunately have to return them. I’ve had them for 2 days and they’ve dropped calls 5 times already and after being on a call more than 10 minutes people have trouble hearing me and say the sound is very muffled. Then 2 separate times today in the middle of a call they put out a very high pitched piercing beeping in my ear that made me have to disconnect the call to make it stop. It was actually painful to me ear.
They were very easy to set up, stayed in my ears well and the sound was great but they’re unusable because of the dropped calls and people having trouble hearing me. Review 2 Rating: 1/5 Title: Don’t buy unless you don’t mind them falling out constantly Text: UPDATE 2: I lost one today when it fell out of my ear into the bushes while doing light yard work. These things suck.

UPDATE: I have lowered my review to 1 star after spending a day doing yard work with these things. I had to continually push them back into my ear while working. I completely regret wasting money on these because of the cheapo rubber tips. There’s no way I’d run with these. Don’t buy these until Anker includes better tips

These are working fine with my IPhone 11 Pro. I’m very disappointed with the rubber eartips though. I’ve tried all of the included sizes and I’m a medium but they are still not staying in my ears for very long. Guess I’ll have to find some better aftermarket eartips but I shouldn’t have to do that. Review 3 Rating: 1/5 Title: UPDATED - FIVE STARS!!! Text: Rec'd a replacement set and they are MARVELOUS! Can't express how happy I am and the customer service I received from the company! I actually gave my son the airpod pros, as these are MUCH more comfortable and the sound quality is on par with the airpod pros! Great product and STELLAR customer service!

Was really excited to get these, as a coworker has them and raves about them. She actually talked me out of getting the air pod pros because these were THAT good. I will say they were super comfortable and noise cancelling was superb! However, after multiple attempts and scouring the internet for help, I couldn't get the left ear bud to work. Never had sound. And getting them to pair with my iphone was a task, ALWAYS. Only had them a few days and sadly will be returning and getting the air pod pros.
summary: Anker's Liberty Air 2 earbuds sound great for music and phone calls. They dropped calls 5 times already and after being on a call more than 10 minutes people have trouble hearing me and say the sound is very muffled. They were very easy to set up, stayed in my ears well and the sound was great.

I purchased these because of their claim about silencing background noise and great mic technology. The first time I used them with my MacBook Air for a Zoom meeting everyone could barely hear me. They said it sounded like I was very far away. I tested this on FaceTime as well via my MacBook with the same result. I then synced them to my iPhone11 and made some sample calls. The actual PHONE CALLS were crystal clear. Overall the performance of the headphones seems better through my iPhone than my MacBook. However, the real issue seems to be with video conferencing. I called Anker to discuss this and they explained that they currently do not have a needed license with Zoom and other video conferencing services which could be the cause of this. Additionally, background noise from my kids could still be heard on the other end. For this reason, I cannot give these headphones a perfect review. I will say, the battery life is outstanding, the comfort level is great, the noice cancellation on my end is decent, and the audio on my end as a user is great. Sadly, the audio for others is not consistent. I did have some syncing issues initially but after a reset I no longer had issues. I'm debating returning these because of their poor performance in the purpose I bought them for. But overall, if you're just looking to make great phone calls and eliminate noise, these will work great for you! However, if you're purchasing them for video conferencing purposes, I would reconsider. Review 3 Rating: 3/5 Title: Ok.... just OK Text: Just OK. The sound in my ears and battery life are excellent.

product_title: Soundcore Anker Life P2 True Wireless Earbuds, Clear Sound, USB C, 40H Playtime, IPX7 Waterproof, Wireless Earphones for Work, Home Office
combined_reviews:
Review 1 Rating: 5/5 Title: Life P2 is C2 for your Ears Text: While i have had Anker Soundcore LifeP2 for about month, I will only speak to how these earbuds have performed thus far. For the month I have had these, I will say this is the best quality earbuds I have had. If Anker charged me $170 for these buds, I would've shelled out the money regardless. Thats how good a product this is. Again Im not much for the idea of something fitting in your budget, because if its important you'll spend what you think its good for you and you want the buds to perform at its best and purest form for at least beyond the length of its warranty. Anker has always made a sound quality product and have set the price for its products that makes it affordable for most if not all.

What i love most about the Life P2 is its versatility. I have used these ear buds to make calls through my Iphone, which i never really did with previous earbuds Ive had because the mic quality on most is garbage, and the ability to hear whoever is on the other line is distant meaning that from an audio perspective the acoustics for voice speaking wasn't adequate and they'd only be good for music.

The Life P2 is good on all fronts. Crisp sound to hear, and clarity to speak.

Ive used the Life P2 for Zoom calls and people have said that my voice is more clear and better than the internal mics that are used the MacBook Airs.

The Life P2 is actually a really solid pair of earbuds for physical activity use & workouts. Since COVID-19 Ive done personal workouts with my Trainer using the P2's via Zoom outdoors and have been able to hear and speak with clarity. That says a lot for me because when you're outside ambient noises distort and distract but no issues there. Their noise cancellation isn't stellar but for me i think its more than adequate for myself and does the needful.

So i feel like Ive put the Life P2 to the test in accordance to my usage-lifestyle.

I will say that the only drawback is the distance. How far away can i move with the earbuds away from my cell before it cuts out. I have noticed that sparingly when my phone is connected and i'm listening to music the earbuds cut out sightly, but it doesn't bother me as much as long as it doesn't shut down automatically or pop on inadvertently like other earbuds i've purchased in the past.

Button Usage - a little sensitive but very responsive and easy to use which is a plus for me

Comfort - Ive found them to be very comfortable. Ive had them on for more than an hour riding my bicycle and have also had them on during my runs. Only a few times do i adjust not from discomfort but because i sometimes feel like it maybe loose. This could be a negative but nothing i don't always do to assure the earbuds are secured in my ears.

Battery Life and Charge - This is a big win for me, Ive said in my previous review of another earbud i purchased regrettably. You cant really give any review or consider a product quality when you cant get a legitimate charge to your earbuds. I don't care how fancy it looks or is built. No charge or issues charging the earbuds is non-starter automatic 1 star product in my opinion. Life P2 blows away the competition. They secure in the case They offer a legitimate charge, and when you automatically remove the buds from the case and add to your ear it tell you if the charge is high or low or will die out. This is a great feature to have. The case gives a solid quick charge in 15 mins for at least an hour. And you get a solid 2 hrs+ on your buds which is huge. It's a legitimate 2+ hrs. I think its more cause i haven't been in a situation that warrants keeping the buds for that long a period but knowing that is a bonus.

Sound Quality- Best in the business as far as Im concerned. Solid on the lows/mids and bass has the kind of punch i like to hear when Im zoning.
Im sure people will lean on Beats or Earpods, SkullCandy or Jabras but Life P2 can run with the big dogs no question here.

Overall- This is as a good an earbuds as i have seen or listened with and it blows my mind how much i spent for these. i have already gotten my moneys worth on these earbuds. You will not be disappointed with these earbuds if you get them. These are a great all-purpose earbuds that will work to accommodate all aspects of your lifestyle and still provide a quality brand and feel your looking for especially if image/look is your thing. Review 2 Rating: 5/5 Title: I'm an Anker man, now! Text: These are so good. I'm really surprised. I've enjoyed some Anker products here and there, but I didn't know they were so firmly on their game!

The waterproofing is great! I love listening to a podcast/audiobook while getting ready, and I just jump in the shower without missing a beat.

I love the charging case. It's small and comfortable in my pocket. It charges the earbuds. Pulling one out turns it on and it quickly/easily connects to my phone.

I've used Bluetooth earbuds that were wired to each other, and I really didn't think "true" wireless would be much better(if at all), but wow I was wrong. One-ear listening is more convenient and the other can charge while one is in use. While both are in it's nice to not fiddle with the wire.

I've never been one to care much about sound quality/clarity. I've always found "good enough" headphones/speakers to be for me: better than super cheapo level, and I've never noticed much of a difference trying more expensive "high quality" ... These have changed my opinion on this! My wife REALLY hasn't cared about sound quality, like emphatically insisting that she just can't tell even between cheapos, but these changed her opinion as well! I had her listen to "Exit Music" by Radiohead and you should have seen her. She was talking about aspects she's never heard before and carrying on. It was kinda great, but now I have to buy her some.

There are more rubber tip choices than I've seen.
USB-C! Like my phone and MacBook!
Sound isolation/cancelling is good.

I got mine on a sale, but I'm buying my wife a set at full $60(not bad!)

I feel I'm gushing almost too much. It's just that they exceeded my expectations. Then there's been a slow burn of pleasantly surprising enjoyment and benefits over the time I've used them.

Another reviewer mentioned a problem with waterproofing. I wouldn't use the buttons while they're wet. The manual didn't say that, but other waterproof electronics have, so I just don't.

I'm still giving 5 stars, but I'll try to point out negatives: Another reviewer mentioned the buttons are a bit stiff and warrant a kinda weird gripping it by the, uh, stem(?) to press without pushing the bud into your ear a bit. I'd forgotten that I'd learned this weird skill, but once he mentioned it, I guess it is kinda awkward.
I think I would've tried to shape the case flatter and wider for less pocket bulge, but again there's not much - it's comfy.
When switching between these and my car Bluetooth, there's bumpiness that I wish went smoother, but that seems to be my car's fault more than the competing devices.
Um, my smartwatch battery seems to be running down faster and MAYBE that has something to do with these earbuds? 🤔

Yeah, I'm grasping, these are just great. 5 Emphatic stars! Review 3 Rating: 4/5 Title: Good Value Text: Been using these for about a week now and I have to say they're pretty good. Sound quality isn't quite as good as the one's that cost $150-$200, but if you download an EQ app they're very close. Not sure on the battery life as I have not used them for more than an hour at a time. Only complaint really is the storage case is a little difficult to get the buds out of. Considering you can buy 3 pairs of these for the price of some Ipods, absolutely worth the money.
summary: Anker Soundcore LifeP2 is the best quality earbuds I have had. If Anker charged me $170 for these buds, I would've shelled out the money regardless. Crisp sound to hear, and clarity to speak. Button usage is responsive and easy to use which is a plus for me.

combined_reviews:
NEGATIVE REVIEWS Review 1 Rating: 1/5 Title: magnet is weak, poor build qualty Text: Overall, the case looks nice and it reasonably protects an iPad Pro. There are two problem, though. First, the magnet is far too weak to lock the case open or closed. The cover will flop. Second, the build of the cover uses a method where a sturdy core is sandwiched between two soft layers. This creates rough edges, and tends to also result in splitting due to the constant bending the edges receive. While I am fully aware of how cheap this case is, I feel other cheap cases I have tried do not suffer from these problems.

Update: 6 months and corners are cracking. Certainly will not last another 6 months.

Final Update: After 7 months, the plastic edges around the corners of the case cracked badly in multiple places. Because of this, the case became unusable and needed replaced (with a different brand). Review 2 Rating: 1/5 Title: Horrible smell l! The back part fits perfect but the flap is big and doesn't always wake or sleep the device so that's an issue as well. The flap is about 2-3cm bigger than the screen device which some people may or may not like. But it's still a nice case and will continue to use which is why I rated 3 instead of 2. I also thought the back would be rubberized but it's plastic. Review 2 Rating: 3/5 Title: Corners crack Text: My initial impression of this iPad case was good. The fit and finish were excellent and the case provided good protection. However, after five months of gentle use at my bedside, with the exception of one month where the case was temporarily replaced with a Logitech keyboard case, the lower corners have cracked and one corner piece has now completely broken away. I expected this case to last for the life of the device it was protecting. I’ll eplace it but not with this product.. Text: This item seemed to be great until I noticed something fishy. When I took this case out of the package it looked awesome. It was the perfect size to my new iPad. I pulled everything out to make sure nothing was inside and I still continued to smell that horrible odor. I then realized that the case itself smelled like rotten fish!!!. I don’t recommend this item at all!!!! Review 3 Rating: 1/5 Title: Oooh That Smell! Text: When I was a child, a squirrel crawled into our air conditioning ventilation unit and died. One day, after using my iPad pro for a while, I went into my postdoc's office and noticed a stinking smell. It reminded me of the dead squirrel from my childhood. I suddenly realized that it was my hand. What the hell? I didn't remember touching anything that stank.

Eventually, I realized it was my iPad pro. But after doing some more sniffing, I realized it wasn't the pro itself. It was the inside of the cover. The part that is covered with a soft material

I tolerated it for a while, but eventually it got worse. And now I can't stand it, so I'm chucking the cover and ordering a different brand.

The other one star reviews have only confirmed that this smell problem is indeed an issue. Although it doesn't seem to be a problem for every cover. The postdoc, who has the same iPad pro case, doesn't have this issue.

product_title: ProCase iPad Air (3rd Gen) 10.5" 2019 / iPad Pro 10.5" 2017 Case, Ultra Slim Lightweight Stand Smart Case Shell with Translucent Frosted Back Cover for Apple iPad Air (3rd Gen) 10.5" 2019 –Rose Gold
combined_reviews:
Review 1 Rating: 4/5 Title: Cover flips all the way to the back Text: I need a simple case for my ipad pro. I don't need a i-pencil holder. I need one that has a front cover to protect the screen and be able to flip easily to the back so I can hold it and play games or watch movies. This case fits all my requirements. My ipad pro fits perfectly into the back cover w/o hassle. Alignment of the holes and buttons are spot-on. Weight of cover plus ipad is still acceptable. When arranged to hold the ipad up, the magnets are not too weak and not too strong, just nice. I confirmed the closing of cover puts ipad on standby; lifting it up turns on the screen. But if I turn the ipad upside down, the front cover will drop because the magnet to trigger standby/ON is not designed to be strong enough to hold the weight of itself. If it is that strong, it will take more effort to flip cover open for normal use and this could be irritating. The cover can be easily flipped to the back and still maintains a flatness which for me is a plus. Some other covers material are so stiff that it is difficult to flip cover to the back or it leaves an unsightly bulge at the bend.

Ok let's be realistic, with the price don't expect a protective case that can survive nukes. It can protect your expensive ipad from occasional scratches and bumps. But if it drops from a table the cover may have a crack,ipad lightly dented. So despite having a light protective case, please be careful. If you want good protection, buy those expensive all-round covers that weigh a ton instead. Review 2 Rating: 5/5 Title: Happy with product Text: This is my second cover. The first died protecting my iPad, so it does its job. Replaced since the integrity had been breached by me dropping my iPad. Both this and my zagg screen protector had to be replaced but my iPad was unscathed. Really like the looks of this cover. Very pretty and professional looking. My one complaint is the inside is some material that I cannot just wipe clean. In fairness I did not try and kind of liquid to clean other than a dampened paper towel. The outside is very easy to clean and stayed looking very nice. Obviously I would buy again as I did to replace the original. Review 3 Rating: 5/5 Title: Five Stars Text: I LOVE this case! It fits perfect, looks awesome and works great! Going to order another one. BTW - you can keep the iPad in the case when you use the Smart Keyboard that goes with it. It's pretty and it folds up and supports the iPad with a little magnet. I had mine personalized and with the see-thru backing, you can see what I put on it - "Mama Bear's IPad" - lol.
summary: Reviewers give it 5/5 stars. The cover can be easily flipped to the back and still maintains a flatness which for me is a plus. The inside is some material that I cannot just wipe clean.

I obviously passed on that offer to give an honest review. My advice, spend the money and get a 120deg view with actual sound and movement alerts to your phone or computer if you really want a good security camera.

It hooks up to our WiFi and we will be ordering more!! Thank you Review 3 Rating: 5/5 Title: Great buy! Text: Great camera, very dependable app attachment, fantastic value. Bought this on a whim because I was going to be out of town for a few weeks, and wanted assurance and peace of mind that my place would be safe and free of intrusion, because there's a lot of movement, turnover and renovations in my complex. After reading mixed reviews here, and horrible reviews on the App Store, I was just keeping my fingers crossed and hopeful that it would work. And by golly, did it ever. Set-up was a breeze for this notorious techie dunce(rolls eyes). And the app never failed to connect with the camera from hundreds of miles away. If I had to come up with one complaint it would be that the notification settings are much too sensitive, and went off constantly when there was no apparent movement. There are 4 sensitivity settings, and it still went off a lot even when I notched it down. Wish there was an on/off button on the camera, for when I'm home and don't need it, then it would be perfection. May even buy a few more to have on hand just because it's unbelievably affordable.

product_title: iXCC Element II Lightning Cable 6ft, iPhone Charger, for iPhone 7 6s 6 Plus, SE 5s 5c 5, iPad Air 2 Pro, iPad Mini 2 3 4, iPad 4th Gen [Apple MFi Certified](White)
combined_reviews:
Review 1 Rating: 3/5 Title: Consistently Fails After About 18 months Text: I have owned more than a dozen and a half of these cables, about half of them in the 3-foot length and half in the 6-foot length.

These seem like good quality cables when they comes out of the package, and I suspect they are faithful to the original Apple design. It may be that the original Apple design is not very robust.

What I have found with every one of these cables is that they consistently fail after about 18 months, for one of two reasons: (a) the cable jacket frays and opens up at the Lightning end of the cable, exposing the inner conductors and eventually shorting out, or (b) the gold plating on the little fingers of the Lightning connector degrades and eventually leads to intermittent connections. In both cases, the symptom becomes obvious when the phone is plugged into a charger and the little "bong" sounds repeatedly. In some worse cases, there is no charging at all.

IXCC states that it offers a two-year warranty for these cables. I have exercised this two or three times in the past, and IXCC promptly sent replacements. However, I have recently sought to invoke this warranty for two cables that I bought at the end of 2016., both of which have failed, and despite two attempts to contact IXCC through Amazon I have received no response.

Thus, I have reduced my previous higher rating of this cable product line to three stars, reflecting the fact that (a) these cables only have a limited life span, and (b) the warranty appears to be meaningless. Anyone buying these cables should simply plan on paying for replacements some time during the second year of ownership.

I am gentle with these cables, and do not extensively flex or kink them. I do plug and unplug them, of course, which causes wear on the gold plating, but I can't help that.

I don't know if any other brand of cable would hold up better than these. Again, it may be that Apple's design is inherently flawed. I simply feel that potential buyers should be aware of the limited life of these cables. Review 2 Rating: 3/5 Title: Lasts about a year Text: I bought two of these in March 2018. I left one in my car and the other was for the house. It’s durable and I was very happy with it for over a year. Last month I noticed my phone charge would drop from 100% to 20% within an hour. I thought it was my battery since I have an iPhone 5. Turns out it was these cords. I bought new ones a few days ago and my battery will last me a full day if not longer now. I don’t want to complain too much since these cords are cheap and I at least got a year, but I hate the waste this cause.

Both cords slowly died off at the same time. The one in my car was left in 100 plus degree temps, but used much less than the house cord. Doesn’t matter how often you use the charger or climate, the chargers only have a life span of a year. Review 3 Rating: 3/5 Title: This cable is great and gets delivered right away Text: This cable is great and gets delivered right away, however it does not fit with an Otterbox Preserver Case, if you have one of these cases do not get this charger.
summary: Cables consistently fail after about 18 months. IXCC states that it offers a two-year warranty for these cables. Anyone buying these cables should simply plan on paying for replacements some time during the second year.

Review 3 Rating: 5/5 Title: The 6ft length is ideal for me, and the cords are thick and feel durable, work great, ordering another Text: As others have commented, the cords seem a bit thicker than my original Apple cords, which is a good thing--makes me think it will be more durable. Title: A Sleek and Gorgeous Case for $25!
Text: [[VIDEOID:mo10ES9ENW4563G]]This is my first Kuzy case and I am amazed with it. My previous case, a Speck has already cracked. Now, I spent $50 on the Speck so with $50, I expect better. The corners of the bottom part of the case has cracked, but it has served its duty. I wouldn't say it's the best $50 I spent but it got the job done. Until the corners started cracking. I started looking for a cheap case because I didn't want to make the same mistake again. I choose the black color because I thought it looked sleek. If you want a better of how the case looks, watch my video. Also, make sure to check out my YouTube channel youtube.com/smchdstudios

Review 2
Rating: 5/5
Title: 5 stars all across the board!
Text: Mind you, I have only had this case for about 20 minutes at the time of this writing, but I already love it. The previous case I used was mCover from iPearl - a popular case on Amazon for a similar price (I got mine on eBay). That one had great reviews, and maybe it's just me, but I didn't like it too much. It definitely did not fit the computer as well as this Kuzy case...I kept having to take it off and clean it and the computer because dirt and dog hair would get in (on both sides) This one is a much tighter fit so I don't see that happening. I probably won't have to take it off unless I want to. That tighter fit also means a slimmer looking Macbook Air (which really is what we all want when we buy the MB Air, isn't it?) Now any case is going to add a bit of weight and bulk to your Air, but the Kuzy case just makes it seem like much less, even though it may not be. The mCase would make a weird noise sometimes when I would open it because it wasn't fit right and I guess the bottom of the case would rub against the top of the computer where you would open it, and I'm so happy to find a case that doesn't do that. (By the way, even though only the 2010 MB Air is listed, this also fits the 2011 MB Air, which is what I have) This just overall seems like a way better made case...I love the feel of it as well, and ofcourse the pretty purple color and the fact that the infamous Apple logo is still visible. The iPearl people were also rude to me when I sent them messages...ignoring me for days even though I saw that they were on eBay because they were leaving customers feedback and my item took quite a while to ship! In comparison, the Kuzy folks responded to my question right away (on a late night!), seemed helpful and friendly, and I got my package sooner than expected. After having the problems with the mCase I thought "Well, you get what you pay for" and was looking into more expensive cases from companies like Speck. Can't say anything about Speck since I haven't owned their products, but many people seemed to have problems with one of the MB Air cases from them, and cases from other companies weren't my cup of tea, so I went with this one and I'm so glad I did (so far). Great case for a fraction of the price. If you are looking for an inexpensive but quality case for your Mac, definitely go with Kuzy!

Review 3
Rating: 5/5
Title: Great! Love the matte feel of it!
Text: Got it in Shell Cover - Pink. true to the color. it has a matte finish very smooth~ all ports are accessible. no fingerprints show up but if you have a little grease or oily hands it will leave finger prints on it. but could easily be wiped off. mine didnt come with the kuzy logo i love it that way better~! apple logo shows thought great!~ top case is very difficult to take off thought! really love this case though!
summary: This is my first Kuzy case and I am amazed with it. My previous case, a Speck has already cracked. If you are looking for an inexpensive but quality case for your Mac, definitely go with Kuzy!

product_title: Wireless Keyboard and Mouse,J JOYACCESS 2.4G Ergonomic and Slim Wireless Computer Keyboard Mouse Designed for Windows, PC, Laptop,Tablet - Black Grey
combined_reviews:
Review 1 Rating: 4/5 Title: It's a keeper for me, great set for the money! Text: [[VIDEOID:3048eeabf820c2c52afc67e1352e8042]] I was hesitant about buying this keyboard and mouse set due to some of the reviews, and the fact that I am very picky about my computer input devices. Honestly, I pulled the trigger based on looks, as I wanted a white and silver set to blend in with a room that was almost all white. There just aren't many keyboards/mice that are this color, so I figured I should give it a go. Well, so far, I am glad I did.

Measurements: The keyboard is approximately 14.5" wide and 5.5 inches tall. The thickness (from your table to the top of the keyboard) is roughly 3/4" at the top of the keyboard, to 1/4" at the bottom, so the keyboard is slightly tilted. The mouse is about 4.5" from top to bottom, and 2.5" from side to side. The height is 1/4" at the front tip of the mouse, approximately 3/4" in the middle, and a bit over 1/2" at the bottom.It is difficult to measure the height, as it is curved to fit the hand, but to me it is a flatter feel that the Microsoft mouse I was using before.I thought this would bug me, but it's actually way more comfortable.

Weight: I don't have a scale for actual weight, but the keyboard is light enough to carry with one hand easily, though not so lightweight that it moves or slides around on my slick wooden table. It does not feel cheap to me, and seems well made. The mouse is very lightweight, slides around my table easily without a mousepad, only has a barely detectable rattle when shaken.

Lag: There is no lag with the mouse at all. It is quick to move around the screen, zips along like a speeding bullet. The keyboard has no lag that I can tell.

Keyboard sounds: Many people have said this is "quiet" and some people have said it's noisy and clacky. To me, it's more of a thunk but not quite. It's not totally quiet, but it's not annoying me either. I'd say it's a matter of tolerance for anyone. I added a video so you could hear the keyboard. My phone is right next to it, and the room is perfectly quiet so you might get a feel for how loud it is. I'd say adjust your speakers for someone talking in a normal volume, then listen to the keys.

Overall Impressions: I like this set. One pro about it is that you only have one usb dongle to use for both the mouse and the keyboard. So if you are short on usb ports, but need both a regular keyboard and a mouse, this is a nice feature. As stated, I am very picky about my input devices. I don't like to hammer on keys, I like them easy to push but not mushy. These keys are springy and responsive. They are slightly hard to push down for me, and my hands and arms get fatigued after long bouts of typing, however, I have muscle, tendon, and joint issues all over my body, so this is not unusual for me.

The keyboard may fall asleep, but I haven't noticed if it has. It has always sprung right to attention when I start using it. The mouse does go to sleep but all it takes is a press down on the scroll wheel or other button and it wakes right up. Better than a dead battery in the morning. There is an on/off switch, but who remembers that? Both keyboard and mouse take AA batteries by the way. Two for keyboard, one for mouse.

The color on the keyboard is an aluminum or silver for the base. The buttons are white, with very dark gray letters and numbers. They have black for the function key and the function symbols like mute, and volume. The mouse is mostly white, with a silver border going around the perimeter and silver accents on the scroll button, along with a small center button I can't seem to figure the use for.

I am using a Windows 10 PC, this works fine. The drivers seemed to install fine, I had no trouble setting it up.

I've just had it a couple of days, but I am pleased enough that it's a keeper. The only thing I feel is missing is a side button that I could use to map a zoom in/out function to, such as my Microsoft mouse has. I really miss that, but otherwise I have had no other misgivings. A solid set for the money in my opinion. Review 2 Rating: 4/5 Title: Not a Multi Device, but nice keyboard/mouse combo Text: The wine red color is not as pretty in person. The image makes it look metallic. It is plastic and a deeper color. I was considering this keyboard because it was a mouse and keyboard combination. It requires a dongle which I'm wondering if you always need that for a bluetooth mouse. You can avoid needing that when you use an apple mouse. I wasn't sure if I wanted the runs on AA batteries feature or not like my current keyboard. My preference is not this and using a rechargeable keyboard instead.

I'm trying out a few multi device keyboards that have number pads. In comparison with iClever, I liked that it also had the buttons or home end page up page down. If you want a full keyboard that is a little less wide, then this is a good feature because it condenses it down to one column.

I'm trying to find the best layout I prefer. This keyboard does type quietly, which is a feature I sought. The first full keyboard multi device keyboard that I have owned for several years now is a Logitech which is very noisy to use in public.

This is a perfectly great keyboard, but just not the layout I seek. Hope this review helps others shopping and making comparisons to what other options are out there. Review 3 Rating: 5/5 Title: Real quite key strike/mouse click operation! Text: I have my laptop connected through an HDMI connection,to my large screen TV in the living room to watch movies,and to fully enjoy such experience,besides a bowel of popcorn, you do need a Wireless keyboard and mouse,for saving trips to TV in case of pausing,searching for different movies.Also,in my line of work,I mainly use laptops,and I could never get used to using the built in pads for the laptop! Right clicking or highlighting is always an issue! Not to mention all the finger prints left on those pads! My alternative is always has been, an external mouse and a keyboard.

Quality/Use:
------------------
The combo comes well packed in a nice protective box,to prevent any damage during shipping! Keyboard and mouse are,plug and play,no software download regardless of the operating system on your computer. The mouse has a good grip and the surface is slip free!l,which means perfect movement and maneuvering.Also the USB receiver is inserted in the battery compartment,for safe keeping,when not in use! One other feature I was impressed with,is the sleep mode of the battery! If you don't use it for a minute or so it will go into sleep mode to preserve energy,and ultimately prolonging the life of the battery.Mouse size is about average for a small and portable mouse.Excellent response time and accuracy in highlighting,with absolutely no lag time!! Keyboard is slim and compact,which can save space on your desk or table. Nice size striking keys,and really quite! Quite keyboard is essential for people that work late at night,when all is quite,the last thing you need is a loud keyboard clicking noise!!
Side numeric keypad is great if you work with numbers.

Final Thoughts:
----------------------
Except for not including batteries with this package(not a deal breaker) This combo set is really unique and will work with both,windows and Mac based computers,which makes it best to be used by students. Beautiful color silver/white combination,that will complement any desktop. After using this combo set for a while,I do like it and recommend it further. As for durability and long lasting,only time and use can tell! Despite the fact that this product was a complementary test sample, I will make sure to come back and report any issues with quality in the future.Still, if you're a skeptical person and not sure whether to trust other people's reviews or not! With Amazon's 30 day return policy, you have four weeks to try it out yourself, before returning it for any reason, with no questions asked, and in most cases Amazon will pay for the return shipping, in other words, it is a win, win situation!! With that said, I hope you find my humble opinion helpful in making or breaking your buying decision.
summary: The keyboard is light enough to carry with one hand easily, though not so lightweight that it moves or slides around on my slick wooden table. The mouse is very lightweight, slides around my table easily without a mousepad, only has a barely detectable rattle when shaken. There is no lag with the mouse at all. The keyboard has no lag that I can tell. The only thing I feel that is missing is a scroll button, along with a small center button.

combined_reviews:
Review 1 Rating: 3/5 Title: Was Hoping for Better Text: Maybe this is on me. It took me a while but I was finally able to get these buds to sound "decent." Not great, but on a letter-grade scale somewhere around a "B." Still not as good as the $50 wired earbuds I was replacing. When I say finally able, that is because it took a lot of trial and error to find the right ear pieces and I was using not only the 4 pair of tips that Phaiser provides but several others I already had. In the end, I was getting the best sound from a large pair of tips that I got at Radio Shack when I lost a tip on an airplane.

Other things I like are the lightness, the remote works well and I am told that my voice is clear on phone calls. The buds come with a large assortment of tips, wings & cable management clips. And of course the lack of wires during the workout is very nice. I did not have any issues keeping the buds in place (I did not even use any of the wings) but I do low-impact workouts.

In the end, what is causing me to return these is the battery life. I have used them during 10 workouts and the best I have achieved (starting from a full charge) is 2 hours and 23 minutes. I can live with having to charge them daily, but sometimes I am at the gym 3 to 4 hours and I can't have my earbuds going home before I do. I know volume can affect battery life, but in order to achieve the 2:23, I kept the volume a click or two lower than I would have liked.

On a side note, when I first purchased these, I received 3 e-mails from 7Desk/Phaiser support saying if I had any issue that would have me considering leaving a less-than-stellar review on Amazon, I should contact them directly. Well, I sent 2 e-mails back detailing the issues I was experiencing and have had no response whatsoever. So I have just printed out my Amazon return label. Review 2 Rating: 3/5 Title: They were OK. The second thing I didn't like about the buds was the design. Maybe it's me but they look bulky in my ear like I had enormous earplug which hurt my ears after prolonged use of the buds. Overall, there great but not for me. Review 3 Rating: 3/5 Title: Good sound. Mic fail Text: Good sound and ear cushion options are good.

product_title: Phaiser BHS-750 Bluetooth Headphones Runner Headset Sport Earphones with Mic and Lifetime Sweatproof Guarantee - Wireless Earbuds for Running, Blackout
combined_reviews:
Review 1 Rating: 5/5 Title: Best BT headphones I've ever owned. Period. Text: I can not find a single thing wrong with these headphones. I work outside around loud equipment and can still hear every word crystal clear from the podcasts I listen to, as well as every note of any music, even classical! I also have not had them fall out for ANY reason, though I am constantly moving myself. Great fit, great custom accessories, and the customer service is exceptional. If you need reliable bt headphones, look no further. You are going to LOVE these. I have run through about four of the cheaper ones, and this just made me a lifetime customer of the Phaiser brand. Would obviously recommend them to anyone.

Update: So within the three months I've had them... battery dies in less than an hour, the left side is cutting in and out, and even fully charged it shuts off for no reason. I've taken good care of them, only to have to buy regular corded headphones. Contacted Phaiser Customer Support, and had a replacement in 3 days, no questions asked. Well done, Phaiser. Well done.

Needless to say, I will not buy any other brand going forward. Review 2 Rating: 5/5 Title: Surprisingly grows on you Text: Honestly, I was disappointed the first time I put these in my ears. I connected them to my iPhone 6 Plus and turned the volume on the phone all the way up, and yet the volume was so faint! Then I realized that the volume control on the Phaiser is independent from the phone; once the earphone volume control was turned up the volume got so loud!

SOUND QUALITY
I wasn't expecting the base to be good at all considering these are wireless. But after using them for a week, and changing the Phaiser equalizer setting to Base (by long-pressing volume up and down for a few seconds), i actually think the base on these are pretty great! I compared them to my old JBL wired earphones. The latter still sound slightly better, but the Phaiser holds up well.

MOBILITY
The main reason I bought these were for mobility, especially while commuting to work. I recently tripped on some steps while switching trains and cracked my phone's screen, and I think the wired earphones were partly to blame because I usually hold the phone in my hand when connected. Now it's so convenient. I just have to hang them around my neck when I leave home, and the magnets keep them securely together. When it's cold and I need to use my ear mufflers, I just have to remove the earphones and hang them around my neck.

BATTERY LIFE
Battery life for me has been about 2 days, with around 4-5 hours of usage. For a full charge, I have to plug it in using the provided micro-USB cable for around 2 hours.

Overall, I recommend these earphones as a great option for people looking to try out wireless Bluetooth earphones. Review 3 Rating: 5/5 Title: Nice sound, Held up to Sweat, and good for runs! Text: I purchased the Phaiser BHS-750 Bluetooth Headphones because my previous purchase from Cambond died for a second time due to sweat. I run indoors and outdoors, and in both situations, I sweat quite a bit. The Cambond headsets did not last after a few grueling and sweaty runs, especially in the South Florida humidity.

While I've been hesitant to write a premature review of the Phaiser BHS-750 Bluetooth headphones, I believe they have met my needs so far.

The headphones came in a nice compact factory-sealed cardboard carton. Inside the packaging was with a nice hard case, 3 pairs (small, medium and large) ear tips, 6 pairs (small, medium and large) ear wing attachments, as USB charging cable, 2 collar clips, 2 pairs of cable management clips, 1 pair of Comply earphone tips T4000, and the user manual. There were lots of useful tips, clips and attachments so you can properly fit the headset to your needs!

The headset was easy to pair with my iPhone. After properly fitting the tips and ear wings, and using the cable management clips to prevent swinging of the additional length of the cable behind my head, I was off and running. The headsets, with the proper ear wings and earphone tips kept the headphones in place for several runs.

While the headphones came with a compact instruction book, I had to refer to an online video/review of the headphones on YouTube in order to learn how to use the cable management clips to prevent the cables from swinging behind my head. When I was cooling down, it was nice to remove the headphones and clip them together using the magnets on the back of the ear units, so I wouldn't lose them.

All in all, these headsets have lasted a few weeks with no static and damage from sweat from my runs! For bluetooth headphones, they sound pretty good too!

Caveat: While the headphones come with Comply ear tips, I would not recommend these for runs outside or workouts where you need to hear the ambient noise of your surroundings. The spongy Comply eartips conform to your ears to block out noise, and are much more suited for environments like inside airplanes.

Keep the Box: While I don't like to keep boxes of items around, the Phaiser box and small bags that came with the headphones was good for storage of all the additional parts for the headphones. If you find the ear tips or wings are too big or too small, you can go back to your box and change them. The carry case has just enough space for the headphones and what you need to carry in your travel bag. You wouldn't want to lose parts if you need them later.

Bonus: The owner, Martin Frischknecht of Phaiser, sends you a welcome/support email and sends you PDF copies of the instruction manual for the headphones.
summary: Review 1: Best BT headphones I've ever owned. Period. Review 2: Surprisingly grows on you. Review 3: Nice sound, Held up to Sweat, and good for runs.

combined_reviews:
Review 1 Rating: 1/5 Title: Disappointed Text: It says in the product description that Amazon publishes that it’s 20 watts of power so I bought it expecting to be blown away...but no, I was instead very disappointed. When you open the package there is a trouble shooting guide to explain why it is that you feel it’s not very loud, well well, so it explains that it’s only 10 watt speakers but there are two of them so that’s why the 20 watts. Hahahaha. That’s not 20 watts, that’s 10. I don’t know why everyone likes it but it has little power and the advert is intentionally misleading given that they obviously know that people expect it to be louder than it actually is. Review 2 Rating: 1/5 Title: Doesn't even turn on after a few weeks of use, sounds like tin can. Text: After just a few uses over the course of three weeks, the speaker will no longer turn on. The only part of it that works now is the charging light. When I did use it, the speaker sounded like it was run through a tin can, metallic and thin. You get what you pay for, but I at least expected to get more than a few uses out of it. Review 3 Rating: 2/5 Title: It is definatly not a 24 Watt output, it's a lot less. Text: I have not take the time to precisely measure the output power, but it is definitely not 24 Watts.

If 100% of battery power could converted to sound, (impossible) the maximum output power would only
be a few Watts.

Here is the analysis:

using a current limited power supply set to 5.0 Volts @ 1Amp, the internal battery can be charged in less
than 4 hours. (It just makes the math very easy to charge with a current limited supply)

5.0 Volts X 1.0Amp X 4 hours = 20 Watt-hours

At full volume, unit can play for about 7 hours (Not very loud, my work environment is very noisy)

So, the 20 Watt-hours are consumed in 7 hours (it plays at constant volume until the end or very close to constant)

20/7 = 2.8571 Watts/hour

So the battery is discharged at a rate of about 2.86 Watts/hour

But the 2.86 Watts is NOT pure sound!!!

It is Power consumed + output sound!!

With 100% efficiency, the best case scenario is 2.86 Watt of output power.
summary: Reviews: 1/5, 2/5 and 3/5. Doesn't even turn on after a few weeks of use, sounds like tin can.

product_title: Vanzon Bluetooth Speaker IPX7 Waterproof Speaker Bluetooth V5.0, Suitable for Party,Travel,Home&Outdoor-Blue (HIJ)
combined_reviews:
Review 1 Rating: 3/5 Title: Needs more volume increments Text: I’d like to compare the Vanzon Climber-Z speaker to the UE Boom 2 LE, which have almost identical form factors, but quite different (retail) prices. Both are somewhat heavy in the hand and crank out the decibels.

Despite its lower price of $59.99, the Vanzon has a fuller sound, i.e., it sounds somewhat better, than the UE Boom 2 LE, and is louder, even at the lower volumes, than the UE Boom 2 LE, AND has a more distinct bass, so I would recommend it if you want or need a louder speaker, such as for larger spaces, or use it outside, where it’s probably great poolside. It is genuinely loud and I only had it up to the 5th or 6th volume setting. OTHERWISE I would recommend the UE Boom 2 LE, since it has much more precise volume control at the lower volumes, which is useful if you tend to prefer your music to stay in the background, or don’t want to bother other people but is still loud enough to be satisfying. In particular, with the Vanzon speaker, between volume setting 2 and 3 the loudness practically doubles, which I found bothersome. There need to be more increments of loudness in this area. Where there are 6 increments of volume at the quiet end with the UE Boom 2 LE, there are only 2 with the Vanzon, so you can’t “fine tune” the volume with the Vanzon in this loudness region, and for me this was a DEAL BREAKER.

Of note is the volume control, which is a dial that rotates at the top of the speaker, which is very convenient.

Note the UE Boom 2 LE is $69.99 at this writing, at Best Buy, marked down from $179.99, while the Vanzon is $59.99, which is its regular price, on Amazon. I had an additional 20% discount at Amazon for the Vanzon. These are extremely good prices for these speakers, both of which deliver extremely good sound. I wouldn’t have paid $179.99 for the UE Boom 2 LE, but considering the added value provided by the app that accompanies it, and the high prices of, for example, the popular Bose speakers, it might just be worth it.

The Vanzon supposedly has EQ settings, but not really. Clicking the On/Off button during play will subdue the highs, but the bass remains the same: strong. The Vanzon vibrates in your hand as you hold it during play.

The UE Boom 2 LE, on the other hand, has an app with actual EQ settings, with 5 presets and 1 custom, and which does seem to provide some control of the music (or podcast). The app is easy enough to use and gives some remote controls, such as for volume, but isn’t necessary to use the speaker. In general, the UE Boom 2 LE is definitely easier to use, even with the app. More thought goes into using the Vanzon, such as pressing the on/off button for 3 seconds to turn on/off, while clicking during play to subdue the highs.

The UE Boom 2 LE has an app, but that isn’t necessary to use.
The Vanzon does NOT have an app.

The sound that plays when the UE Boom 2 LE is turned on can be turned off with a setting in the app, which is fortunate because these sound effects are often obnoxious and loud and the main reason I sometimes return bluetooth speakers. The Vanzon also has an on/off sound effect, which isn’t too obnoxious, however it can’t be turned off but this shouldn’t be a deal breaker. I wish they could do away with these strange sound effects entirely. Or maybe a simple, but very quiet, musical note, rather than the obnoxious noises that seem to be the norm for bluetooth speakers. Review 2 Rating: 3/5 Title: Speaker is decent when volume is cranked Text: This speaker was decent when the volume is cranked up. But anything under 1/2 volume didn't sound good at all. I was looking for a speaker to use at work and didn't want to have it cranked up all the time, really the majority of the time I need it to be under 1/2 volume. I ended up returning this speaker and getting a JBL charge. It does sound good if you have the volume up, the battery life was decent,and it's pretty cheap as well. Review 3 Rating: 3/5 Title: Good sound. Blue tooth an issue Text: Great sound! But you can only sync one device at a time. So if someone else’s phone is connected to the Bluetooth speaker, you can’t connect a different phone until you disconnect the previous one. Which is really inconvenient if that phone isn’t around.
summary: Despite its lower price of $59.99, the Vanzon has a fuller sound, i.e., it sounds somewhat better. The Vanzon vibrates in your hand as you hold it during play. Clicking the On/Off button during play will subdue the highs, but the bass remains the same: strong.

My other tiny issue with these speakers is that it's not intuitive to sync them to the bluetooth on my phone.

connect.
OVERALL, I LOVE THESE SPEAKERS, and the two issues I have are very minor. I recommend these speakers

Review 2 Rating: 4/5 Title: unbiased review of the router - including opinion of business practices Text: I don't often leave reviews either good or bad about items, but in light of Medialink's threats to sue the reviewer with the top helpful negative review on amazon, I figured I would weigh in.

I understand the vitriol at which people are responding to Medialink's marketing strategy. It effectively is marketing through deception, and I for one was deceived. I place a lot of stock in customer reviews, and with the possibility that many of them are not real, and that negative reviews may have been removed because of coercion by the company makes me feel very disenfranchised regarding my decision to purchase this router in the first place.

I own this router and its older brother the 150Mbps model. I replaced the 150Mbps model for the same reasons I see in many of the newly posted complaints. Strong signal strength throughout my 3 bedroom 1400 square feet apartment, but transfer rates diminished astonishingly the further from the router I got. My Roku in the living room constantly lost connection and had streaming issues, and forget about browsing on the ipad while in bed.

This model with 300Mbps and dual antennas completely resolved those issues for me. I have strong signal throughout the apartment and I don't notice transfer speeds suffering from anywhere inside and even a reasonable distance away from my apartment. The router isn't centralized, so even at the furthest point away inside still gets good speeds.

Although I'm in IT, I like to keep work at work and I don't really care about all the numbers until things don't work well enough for me to notice and be annoyed with. This router works well enough for me and I don't intend on replacing it unless it decides to crap out in the near future.

I also won't be buying another Medialink product. I probably would have based on my experiences with this router and the customer reviews... but now that I know they can't be trusted, I'll probably start vetting companies with more scrutiny before making a purchasing decision in the future.

Normal surfing/ buffering had no noticable problems. I have had multiple techs out to find a problem with the cable service. I did not want to spend the money on one of them, So, I thought I would try this one. It works great. I now get great WiFi downloads on my very old Dell laptop (19Mb download, and 5MB uploads) My hardwire connections get 48MB up and 5 MB down. I am very happy with this modem. I have 7 other routers I can see in my neighborhood and have No conflicts with them either.
