combined_reviews:
Review 1 Rating: 1/5 Title: Lasted 6 months!! Text: This TV was fine for the first 6 months. Then, the HDMI ports stopped producing video from the cable box. I tried different HDMI cables and had same problem. Then, I hooked up another TV to the cable box and that worked fine so I know it was not a cable box issue. By process of elimination, it had to be the HDMI ports on this TV that stopped working. Very disappointed that I have to buy another TV now. Would not recommend Review 2 Rating: 1/5 Title: Regret this purchase. Glitches. Loud remote Text: Over a year update. Horrible. I completely regret this purchase. This is my first fire stick TV and I wish I had bought a Roku TV. All of my other Roku TVs and Roku devices have lasted years but this fire stick TV is a horrible viewing experience. The screen is great is visually very clear but the functionality is horrible. No matter what app I watch it suddenly closes in the middle of viewing-Hulu, HBO, and Netflix all will suddenly close in the middle of viewing. Sometimes the apps will not open again and even if they do open they will close very quickly again and I have to just watch something else on another app. I will never buy another fire stick TV. I wish I had listen to the reviews. I have reset the TV to factory settings many times and that does not work.

3 month update: I am not happy with this purchase. The tv glitches so badly. I thought it was perhaps the app itself but it’s not. It glitches on all apps. I’ll be watching a show and the tv will just go to the Home Screen. The remote almost never reacts properly. I’ll press a volume button and nothing will happen. Then minutes later the volume will finally respond. I have to press buttons twice to get them to respond. My Samsung tv of 10 years that I bought to replace this is still running much better than this one.

Despite reviews I bought them. It’s a great event. Picture and sound are great. The remote is loud. Like pushing the buttons is strangely loud. The tv does not want to power off. Push the power button and the screen goes black and then it turns back on. Push the power button again and it asks if you want to power off, I click yes and the screen goes back and then the tv turns back on. Hit the power button again and it finally turns off. Weird. But it’s light. Super easy to put the sturdy legs on. Overall I recommend. Review 3 Rating: 1/5 Title: Alexa is a selfish controlling biatch Text: This TV is crap crap crap crap. !! It will not let me watch anything on Hulu! I've tried over and over and over and as soon as the movie starts within three minutes it stops and goes directly to the Amazon prime home screen! it will only let you watch movies and shows on Amazon prime!! forget trying to watch Hulu or Netflix or anything else and if you press the volume it will make it go back to the home screen !!! it's crap!!! I never did like Alexa! Google is much mire compatible!
summary: Review 1: This TV was fine for the first 6 months. Then, the HDMI ports stopped producing video from the cable box. This is my first fire stick TV and I wish I had bought a Roku TV. The screen is great is visually very clear but the functionality is horrible.

product_title: Toshiba 32LF221U21 31.5-inch Smart HD 720p TV - Fire TV, Released 2020
combined_reviews:
Review 1
Rating: 3/5
Title: Laggy, slow to change channels, navigate apps
Text: I have several fire TV 4k sticks and thought this TV would be great to just have it built in, but no, sadly it is very slow and laggy compared to the firetv stick. It takes way to Long to tune to a channel and opening and closing apps is very slow as well. I wish I had just gotten a regular TV and plugged a fire TV 4k stick into it. The picture quality on the TV is great, so it's a real shame that the interface is so slow. I was also disappointed to find out the Ethernet port is only 100mbs and not gigabit speed. I feel like in today's world, especially for a streaming device, gigabit Ethernet should be standard.

Review 2
Rating: 3/5
Title: You get what you pay for
Text: This was part of the Prime Day deals and a great price. The TV is extremely easy to set up and use, and weighs almost nothing. You do need to put the feet on the TV if you're not mounting it to the wall, which requires a phillips head screwdriver. The picture quality and sound are both fine. Compared to the 10-year-old TV it was replacing, it's only slightly better, which is either an indication of how good my old TV was, or how little the industry has progressed, at least in the smaller sized TV realm. Shipping was faster than expected and arrived safely. If you're in the market for a small, reasonably priced smart TV, I would recommend this one.

Review 3
Rating: 3/5
Title: Good TV, but an issue for Spectrum subscribers
Text: The TV works well -- good picture and sound -- and the price is right, but there's a significant caveat. It doesn't accept the Spectrum app, so doesn't work well if that's how you get your cable service. We were able to work around this by attaching an Apple TV device, but that's an added cost. Probably would have bought a non-"Fire edition" TV if we'd known before we got it.
summary: The TV is extremely easy to set up and use, and weighs almost nothing. The picture quality and sound are both fine. It doesn't accept the Spectrum app, so doesn't work well if that's how you get cable service.

product_title: Toshiba 32LF221U21 31.5-inch Smart HD 720p TV - Fire TV, Released 2020
combined_reviews:
Review 1 Rating: 5/5 Title: Why did I wait so long? Text: I decided to get a TV for my bedroom, and after a lot of research between all the 32” TVs with either built-in Fire or Roku, I decided on this one and am so happy I did.

I’m a woman of a certain age - old - and am technologically challenged, but setup of this TV was a breeze, starting with clear instructions on how to take it out of the box. (The screen came in a plastic bag with two handles, and it just slid out of the box.) The picture and sound quality are fantastic.

There were a lot of questions in the Q&A section about Bluetooth. I got the 2020 version of this TV, and you CAN connect things with Bluetooth. It’s an option in Settings. I connected AirPods, and they work perfectly. Once connected, the speakers on the TV cut off, the remote’s volume control works on the AirPods, and they stayed paired with the TV so I don’t have to go through the process of pairing every time I turn on the TV. Now when I turn the TV on, the TV’s speakers come on until/unless I take my AirPods out of their charging case; if I put them back in while watching, the TV’s speakers automatically kick in. Couldn’t ask for it to work more smoothly!

I have a Mohu Leaf Indoor Antenna on order; it was actually supposed to come today, but when I checked my Amazon orders for today’s deliveries, it’d been changed to next Saturday. No biggie; I know the antenna will work because I already bought one for another TV - picked up 147 channels.

I added Roku to get Peacock TV which isn’t available on Fire right now. This is where I made a mistake. After doing what I thought was pretty thorough research, I got the Roku Express. Turns out it doesn’t connect to 5GHz, only 2.4 GHz. It seems to be working okay, but I’ve ordered the Roku Streaming Stick+ which will connect to 5GHz. (Those numbers really don’t mean anything to me. I just know 5GHz = good, 2.4GHz = not so good.)

The remote for the TV isn’t exactly as pictured; it has a Disney+ dedicated button instead of HBO Max; okay with me. I wish the remote had lighted keys - that’s my only VERY minor complaint. Other than that, the remote is fantastic - so much so that I ordered the 3rd Generation Fire Stick which has the exact same remote to replace my old Fire Stick (and remote) on the living room TV.

I started this journey because I got sick of paying $100 a month to Dish when all I basically did was watch local and network news (and Prime TV). I got the Mohu antenna ($40) for my living room TV which took care of the news. This Toshiba TV is just icing on the cake. I was just THINKING about getting it, but when the price dropped by $50 (to $150), I decided to take the plunge. I ended up getting Hulu (no ads) for $12/mo and Peacock TV (no ads) for $10/mo. After the initial cost of all the hardware I bought, I’ll be saving ~$80 a month, so I’ll be coming out ahead in no time. I’ll probably add more channels/apps in the future, but for now I have plenty of new stuff to watch.

I wish I had done this long ago, but I didn’t even know it was possible. Dish was going to raise my rates again (by $25/mo) and I’d just had it, so I started checking into things. To give you an idea of how long I’ve been overpaying, my Dish equipment was so old they didn’t even want it back. I feel so much better now that I’m not paying $100 a month to watch the nightly news AND I have an awesome new TV in my bedroom!

Update: I’ve had these TVs (I bought two) for two months. Peacock+ is now available on Fire TV, so no need for the Roku stick anymore. My Mohu Leaf antenna is connected, and is working very well; I can change to the antenna output by choosing the option onscreen. I’m still very happy with my purchases. Review 2 Rating: 4/5 Title: Tv has a nice picture Text: Quick set up i hope i don't have to return it the remote is not working it stopped after the first day called Toshiba i was on the call for a hour before they decided to send a new remote in hopes that it works and its not the tv. Review 3 Rating: 4/5 Title: EASY TO MOUNT ON WALL MOUNT AND EASY TO HOOK UP Text: The only complaint I have is how many steps you have to go through to get to the Sleep feature. I use this feature a lot and there was a button on my last TV remote to access it, on this one the only way to get to it is through the settings feature and you must do this before you start watching your preferred program. I tried to access through Alexa and it doesn't recognize what I am asking for. Other than that, this TV is perfect for what I want.
summary: Toshiba's new 32” TV has a built-in Fire or Roku device. The remote is fantastic - so much so that I ordered the 3rd Generation Fire Stick. My Mohu Leaf Indoor Antenna is working very well; I can change the antenna output by choosing the option on the screen.

product_title: Plantronics BackBeat Fit Wireless Headphones Plus Smartphone Armband - Retail Packaging - Green
combined_reviews:
Review 1 Rating: 5/5 Title: READ ME IF THEY DON'T FIT PERFECTLY OR YOU WANT MORE CLARITY AND BASE!!!! Text: Review:
Best Bluetooth headset available because of fit, sound and ability to STAY in your ears and around your head.
Headset DOESN'T bother back of neck and essentially FLOATS around your head.
MUCH better than other loose cord headsets like Jaybirds that constantly fall out. This cord if just flexible enough and not too stiff.
Sound is GREAT for this price range and can be even BETTER if you follow this 1 suggestion

1. BUY "ENDURANCE SERIES ENHANCERS SIZE 5", THEY ARE ONLY $10 FOR 2 PAIRS FROM JBL!!! Size 5 is the most common size and fits right over the stock BackBeat bud. This Enhancer bud extends the "tube" that goes into your ear canal and allows for a more secure and solid connection. This connection increases EVERYTHING from BASE to TREBLE and just overall fit. There are a few different types of "Enhancers" so you can experiment with them. In order to get them to fit over the BackBeat bud you have to cut a little slit in the back for the "loop" to go through. Honestly some of the smaller Enhancers can all out replace the stock BackBeat bud instead of modifying it but both together fits best for me. To get the cut right just line the "tube" up on both buds than mark on the Enhancer where the "loop" comes out than make a SMALL single cut slit. Put the Enhancer on top of the bud than pull the loop through, this should help make the slit bigger without causing you to make to large of a hole. IMO its worth it for $5 a piece to experiment with this. I am including a few pictures to help explain what I have done here.

Conclusion: This additional purchase/modification has turned these 9.5/10 headphones into perfect 10's for me. I love them so now I just hope they last :-) Review 2 Rating: 4/5 Title: Fantastic with one small flaw Text: I've had these for 8 months. They're fantastic: Long battery life; comfortable to wear; stays in the ear through vigorous workouts; great sound. The only reason I gave it 4 stars is the plastic coating around the ear is starting to peel (see picture). Otherwise, the headphones are in perfect condition. Review 3 Rating: 4/5 Title: Almost perfect! Text: Pros:
- The design allows you to hear ambient noise even when listening at higher volumes
- Stays in the ears without falling out during runs
- So light you forget you even have them on
- Sweat-proof, unlike the LG HBS-750 headset that these replaced
- Excellent customer service; my first pair started buzzing out of the right speaker. A quick call later, my new pair were on their way (I didn't have to send mine back first) and I received them only a couple of days later.
- Long battery life; I can run an entire week (25 - 30 miles) without recharging; I'm running in a marathon soon and have no doubt these will last the entire four hours or so

Cons:
- First pair started buzzing after about nine months of use, though luckily they were still under warranty and I was shipped a new pair at no cost
- The two control levers on either side are so small they're difficult to use, particularly if you want to adjust volume while running. It's virtually impossible to do that without hitting the larger pause button. See photo to get an idea of how small these little levers are.
- The sound is OK, I think mostly due to the fact that these intentionally sit loosely (but securely) in the ear. The bass isn't nearly as good as are proper-fitting earbuds. I've purchased (but not yet installed) some JBL ENDURANCE SERIES ENHANCERS SIZE 5 that I expect will improve the sound. However, I also like hearing ambient sound for safety reasons, so this particular "con" exists for a good reason.
- Mildly uncomfortable on really long runs
summary: Bluetooth headset is one of the best in its price range. Long battery life; comfortable to wear; stays in the ear through vigorous workouts. Sweat-proof, unlike the LG HBS-750 headset that these replaced.

product_title: Amazon Basics 3-Button Wired USB Computer Mouse, Black - Pack of 30
combined_reviews:
NEGATIVE REVIEWS Review 1 Rating: 2/5 Title: (Edited) Cheap, in both senses of the word Text: Original review:

This mouse is cheap...in both senses of the word.

There's nothing wrong with it, mind you. I tend to think simpler is better in terms of mice, so I wasn't out to get anything fancy, and this definitely fits the bill. It's slightly smaller than my last one, both in length and thickness, but it's not as little as those awful miniature ones. And it works exactly like it ought to.

It is, however, super chintzy. It doesn't have nearly the heft or sturdiness of my last mouse; it feels like I could crush it just by squeezing too hard. If I hadn't somehow busted the scroll wheel in my last (HP brand corded) mouse, there's no question that I'd prefer to keep using the old one. If I'd known just how cheap this one felt before buying it, I might have paid a few extra bucks for another HP one.

But I didn't, and as I said, there's nothing wrong with this mouse. It works fine, and I'll keep it and use it. If you don't mind having an extra light weight mouse, then definitely get this -- you can't beat the price. If you would prefer something a NEUTRAL REVIEWS Review 1 Rating: 3/5 Title: Three Stars Text: Very basic mouse with no frills. It's a bit on the small side, which can sometimes lead to hand cramps.

Today, less than 2 years since my purchase, my middle mouse button ("clicking" the scroll wheel) broke. It still makes the clicking sound, but I have to press really hard to get it to actually click. Sadly it seems to be a case of a faulty microswitch. Other than that, it seems to be working fine.

I've had other mice that cost nearly ten times as much as this one that also suffered from faulty microswitches and needed to be replaced within 2-3 years, so I can't complain too much about it breaking in under 2 years since it's so inexpensive to replace. Even so, the middle mouse button isn't used very often, so in theory it should last the longest.

In summary:

It will do the job, and it won't hurt your wallet too much when you inevitably need to replace it a couple years down the line.Works like it should. Nothing fancy Review 2 Rating: 3/5 Title: Great mouse for great price Text: I don't care for the fancy gaming mice available nowadays. Give me a basic optical mouse with 2 buttons and a scroll wheel POSITIVE REVIEWS Review 1 Rating: 5/5 Title: Quite a mouse for the price! Love it. Text: I read some complaints in the reviews here that the mouse is very small to them. I don't have very big hands, but I expected something abnormally small. This mouse feels just fine to me. I love the feel of it both in the texture it's made out of and in the size and shape. It's not a gaming mouse so I don't expect it to be at the same par as one, but honestly it works flawlessly for such a great price and I would go so far to say that you could game with this mouse and have a good time. I am using this mouse right now on my laptop. I did not buy it with the intent to game with it but if my gaming mouse does take a crap I would definitely consider swapping it out with this mouse while I wait for a replacement. Just a really great purchase. I showed it to a friend and he said "wow this is what you get for $6.99 from Amazon? This is better than the mice they buy for us where I work." He's probably right, it's a very solid mouse in all the right ways.

Update: I gave this mouse
summary: This mouse is cheap...in both senses of the word. If you don't mind having an extra light weight mouse, then definitely get this -- you can't beat the price. It's a bit on the small side, which can sometimes lead to hand cramps.

He's probably right, it's a very solid mouse in all the right ways.

Update: I gave this mouse to someone that needed it more than me and they are now using it as their main mouse. I bought myself another one for my laptop as my travel mouse and it's been excellent to have in my life again. Review 2 Rating: 5/5 Title: Great little mouse Text: I have to say this is an awesome mouse for the price. I game quite a bit on my PC and for about 2 years I was using a Razer Deathadder Black. It was a great mouse, and I highly recommend it. Recently the right click on it had been acting up and not really working that well. I decided to save some money and instead of replacing it with something expensive, went with this mouse after reading some of the reviews for it. I am extremely happy with the purchase. Sure, it's a very simple mouse, but I don't really notice any huge difference in reaction in terms of movement and actions, and I love the pseudo rubberized feel to it - very similar to my Razer. It feels very soft in the hand and it pretty comfortable despite my larger hands (I mainly play with a claw grip on my mouse anyways).

I would highly recommend this mouse, you can't beat the price, and it's a good quality no frills mouse. Review 3 Rating: 4/5 Title: Ordered 25, and would order more. Text: I ordered 25 of these for my classroom in school. The price was right to have a bunch of 10 year olds mess with them and we needed something other than those built in track pads the come on laptops.Below is my (knit-picky) review of these basic mice.

Packaging: They come in Amazon's frustration free packing I believe. They were very well packaged (zip ties, bagged, and in a cardboard box). Easily opens, but with 25 this ended up taking a bit of time.

Compatiability: So far these plug and play mice have worked within a few seconds on every device tested. This includes a MacBook, Dell Laptop running Windows, several desktops, and even my Raspberry Pi running Linux. No complaints here.

Build: Both the right click and left click have a good feel to them. Lots of "clickiness" without too much resistence. Scroll wheel works well, but to be very picky for 10 year olds I think it could have a bit more resistance. Nice thin rubber coating adds a small level of protection to the mouse while adding a bit of grip. Some mice I've tried have relatively short cords. This mouse has a nice long cord that can easily reach from a desktop to where you need it. Lasers all work well and as expected, and sure seem to out last the old mouse ball design.

The size is a bit smaller and slimer than what I would consider to be average (compared in photo). I still think it's comfortable to use, but if you have bigger hands it is something to consider. The smaller size actually ended up being a plus for me, as my students tend to have smaller hands.

Of the 25, they have all survived daily classroom/child for about 5-6 months now, so I think they are pretty durable. No issues have occured.

For my use, I am rating them 4 stars. While I have no complaints, if I was buying one mouse for constant home use I would spend a few extra dollar and get something a little nicer.
summary: Review 1: "I love the feel of it both in the texture it's made out of and in the size and shape" Review 2: "You can't beat the price, and it's a good quality no frills mouse" Review 3: "The smaller size actually ended up being a plus for me, as I tend to have smaller hands"

product_title: MTX Audio RT8PT Universal Powered Subwoofer Enclosure
combined_reviews:
Review 1 Rating: 5/5 Title: As good as it gets for your dollar Text: I'm a certified installer from back in the day. My past rarely allows me to settle on mediocre sound in my vehicles. Bass in a system is the filler that adds depth and dimension to your sound. I made the decision to run a larger than needed sub box to sound good at low volume (doesn't need to struggle) and because I have the room in my old beater van. Plus because this is in my beater I didn't want to spend much but ended up with way more than expected. I remember needing my customers to spend at least $1500 for a total system to get the sound this has.

This MTX terminator sounds perfect. I went with this mainly because it is a sealed box. Sealed enclosures tend to work with all music types. Not too boomy or muddy. Band pass and sealed have been my favorites.

Teaming a heavy duty enclosure with a quality amp is a must. I went with the Rockford Fosgate R500X1D. Good amps are always underrated. The Rockford Fosgate R500X1D is rated at 500 watts into 2 ohm but registered at 631 watts! Proper wiring is must. I used 4 gauge high quality power cable. Be careful with wiring these days, many companies lie about wire size. They say 4 gauge but you end up with 6 gauge wire with a 4 gauge jacket. Go with a good brand and expect to pay good money. It will be worth it. Also notice in the picture, I added 4 gauge quick connect for power and ground so I can quickly remove the box if I need extra room for moving items.

The team is a perfect match. Playing the police to slipknot sounds unreal. Very smooth controlled low end with a good punch you can feel. No noticeable coloration or distortion.

You will love this enclosure for sure. Review 2 Rating: 5/5 Title: Amazing!!! Text: If your on a budget but you want good subs, go with these. They hit so hard, and when I drive around I set car alarms off so im happy. Review 3 Rating: 5/5 Title: More than you'll need Text: I was so impressed with my MTX terminator [[ASIN:B003E35XGQ MTX TNP212D2 Dual 12" Subwoofer And Amplifier]] install that I did in my '92 Suburban that when I went looking for a new amp/sub combo for my Acura, of course I would check what MTX has to offer. I would have just bought another terminator kit, but this was cheaper and smaller (2x12 in the trunk would have been a tight squeeze).

Setup was very straightforward and easy, and although the instructions could have been a little more in-depth, I realize that this kit is intended for novices who don't know a lot about car audio and providing more information would have ultimately been more confusing to most people rather than helpful.

I decided to go with an amp/sub combo because there are many factors that go into creating a good amp/sub combo, which includes subwoofer size, how many subwoofers to hook up, how to wire your subs, what impedance level to achieve, etc. It makes no sense to buy an expensive set of subs, with a cheap amp, nor a cheap set of subs with an expensive amp. I feel like these combos are matched for both price and performance and you won't be losing out on either, not to mention it's much simpler (and much cheaper) to just buy it all in a combo deal like this.

You really get a lot for your money with this kit, it doesn't feel or look cheap at all. The sub box is embroidered, is very heavy and appears to be really well built. The amp has a blue LED "cross hair" power-on light that is a nice touch.

Trust me, you'll have more way more than enough bass for any type of listening at decibels that aren't causing you severe hearing loss. There is enough bass in this combo to completely saturate and overpower your entire speaker setup (why you would want to do that, I have no idea, but you could if "your thing" is only hearing bass when you listen to music). Someone recently reviewed that his/her 6x9 speakers have more bass than this setup, clearly he/she hadn't hooked up this unit correctly (or even at all perhaps?). The bass response of 6x9" speakers is no where in the same galaxy as this setup.

I am in no way shape or form an "expert" on car audio by any means, but I have done quite a bit of playing around and researching so I do have a couple of tips when setting up your system for the first time:

1. If your head unit only has 1 RCA subwoofer out, simply connect that subwoofer out to either the (L) or the (R) input on your amp. This is a monoblock amp and doesn't utilize stereo separation. You can use a splitter if you want to achieve the same thing (but why would you?)

2. Setting your gain level is important to ensure undistorted playback as well as maintaining a healthy life of both your amp and your speakers. Proper technique for achieving the utmost gain without distortion is done with an oscilloscope, but the quick and dirty way is to set your head unit to the max volume that you will be listening to your music at (which if you'd like to continue using your ears for hearing, shouldn't be distorting or anywhere near that level). While your music is playing at that level, raise the gain on your amp until you can hear distortion in the bass, as soon as you can hear that, back it off. It's better to err on the side of lower gain for reliability.

3. Setting your LPF (low pass filter) is important. You want your subwoofer to only amplify the frequencies that fall into the region of "bass level frequencies", having your sub responding to the full spectrum of frequencies (high's and mid's) will leave your music sounding very "muddy" and unclear. To set this properly have a song playing that you are familiar with, set the LPF at zero, then slowly start to turn the LPF up to where the sub is responding to the kick-drum and bass of your music. Keep raising the LPF until you can hear it starting to pick up higher frequencies (no longer JUST the kick-drum and bass), then roll it back down a bit. I have mine set around 85hz which to me sounds the best, but again, it's a personal preference and should be set to your specific taste. Most modern head units will offer you a lot of tuning options for LFP and HPF for a good separation.

4. Don't be afraid to just have fun exploring, you'd be surprised the world of a difference just a few adjustments can make.

I hooked everything up with Knuconceptz's wiring kit [[ASIN:B0050I6KII KnuKonceptz KCA Complete 4 Gauge Amp Installation Kit]] which was cheap, high quality and was more than what I needed to wire up this setup.

One last thing that is very important, MTX provides amazing customer service. I emailed them with a technical question and they got back to me within 3 minutes! They were very informative and helpful and they really care about you enjoying your MTX product, that alone is worth them getting my hard earned money. If you're having trouble figuring something out, email them or give them a call and you'll be happy with the response and technical knowledge you'll receive.

If you've never had an amp/sub in your car, do yourself a favor and get it, you won't be disappointed.
summary: The Rockford Fosgate R500X1D is rated at 500 watts into 2 ohm but registered at 631 watts! Proper wiring is must. Be careful with wiring these days, many companies lie about wire size.

combined_reviews:
Review 1 Rating: 2/5 Title: Unreliable Text: I bought three of them because of the low price and good user reviews. One of them simply doesn't work (no sound), and the other two mostly work, but have reliability problems (static noises and disconnections with movement). The jack/plug connections are loose, and can disconnect too easily.

Others seem to have had better luck with this product, and you may too, but I can't recommend it. To me, it has been a waste of time. Crappy products lowers people's life quality. Buyers (saying this to myself also) should pay for quality to give manufacturers who care about quality a chance. Review 2 Rating: 1/5 Title: Get something that works instead of this! Text: This is ridiculous. This is the first time in my life that a simple audio cable did not function right out of the package. I plugged it into my TV, and could barely hear anything through my headphones. If I turned the volume up, I could hear the background music and sounds, but the voices were very distorted. (Plugging headsets directly into TV eliminated these problems.) The female plug is also off. It requires a very tight snap in of either of my headsets. I feel like it may even break the headset plugs. Don't waste your time. Review 3 Rating: 1/5 Title: I was sure the male end would be standard sized ... product_title: iMBAPrice® 25 Feet Professional Quality Nickel Plated 3.5 mm Male/Female Stereo Audio Extension Cable
combined_reviews:
Review 1
Rating: 3/5
Title: Know what type of headphones you want to use this with, and make sure they will work! (Only TRS, 2 black lines, will work)
Text: This product works great, but you need to be wary of what headphones you're using it with! I didn't realize this only works with TRS headphones (meaning there are only 2 black lines on the male end of your headphones, see image) instead of TRRS headphones (which have 3 black lines). If you have headphones that have a microphone capability, they will be TRRS headphones and will not work with this. To get them to work, you can buy a TRRS Headset To TRS Headphones Adapter (I got some here: https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00MV2PDCA/ref=oh_aui_detailpage_o01_s00?ie=UTF8&psc=1), but that is an additional cost I wasn't expecting. iMBAPrice should warn users about this before purchasing.

Review 2
Rating: 3/5
Title: Three Stars
Text: feels super thin and cheap, which it is.

Review 3
Rating: 3/5
Title: cable
Text: Product arrived with an exposed slice through the outer cover on the male end of the cable. Bare wire can be seen for about a half an inch. Cable works fine but I need to repair so the cable will last. Any ideas? Thank you for your help.
summary: Reviewers gave the product three out of five stars. Reviewer: Cable arrived with an exposed slice through the outer cover. iMBAPrice should warn users about this before purchasing.

product_title: Beams MB3000 High Performance 500 Lumen Wireless Battery Powered Motion Sensing LED Dual Head Security Spotlight, Brown, 1-Pack
combined_reviews:
Review 1 Rating: 5/5 Title: Big light in a little package! Text: [[VIDEOID:48cdda1357d81c9ed3d88cd6f3dd0c83]] We have been looking for a non-electrical porch light, as we have no outlet, and when I had the chance to review these, I figured there was nothing to loose, since you get 2 for the price of 1!

The lights DO require FOUR D size batteries, and it is recommended to put in brand new ones.The back is easy to open, you can use a dime and unscrew all 4 points to open the battery compartment.Note- unscrew ALL the way, the screws will not fall out, but will be hinged,so to speak. Once you put the batteries in, make sure you select which lighting preference you want- the selector is under the compartment. A bit of a pain, but I get why it is there. Then you just rescrew the cover on and place the light in a dark from for about 10 minutes, to induce the sensor to start working.

Once we put the light outside it took 2 different tries of passes before it started working, but once it did, WOW. This light is BRIGHTER than any of the sensor lights we have outside the house already! It easily picks up when someone opens the porch door and comes on, providing plenty of illumination to see to the front door, and unlock it. In fact you get about 15 seconds more light once you get in, before it automatically goes off. And it is not super sensitive, ie moths do not set it off! However repeated movement will keep it lit!

Because it is SO bright we ended up ONLY needing one on the porch, and the other we were able to put on the carport. Now between the 2 there is more than enough light to safely get in the house, and to see anyone who might be lurking outside!

We highly recommend these little lights- they are perfect for placing where YOU need light!

I was super surprised with JUST how well these battery powered lights work!

UPDATE:

The batteries lasted about 10 months, thanks to a squirrel and bird who both got on the porch and made nests. UGH. their motion constantly set off the lights, hence the batteries didnt last the year as expected..These are the BEST motion lights we've EVER had! Review 2 Rating: 4/5 Title: Perfect setup for my parents house/shed. Text: My dad had asked me if we could figure out something for some motion sensing lights on the side of their house and the shed. I initially said lets run a solar panel or low voltage wire to a transformer and wire things up. This was going to be more work than what was needed for his purpose.

He wanted to have a light come on at the bottom of the yard when their dog goes out so that can see him. He also wanted one on the side of the house so that when one walks up or down the steps it's illuminated so you don't fall.

I found these and we had ordered them. I'm not a HUGE fan of anything taking batteries as that is something that will always need replaced, which in my opinion is quite annoying, on top of if the batteries start to leak or destroy the light itself internally. However, for his purpose he was completely OK with having to change the batteries once or twice a year (depending on how often they turn on).

Honestly I thought these would be a joke. But, I am glad I was wrong. Surprisingly they are quite bright. If you walk by they pick you up from about 20' or so. It's too soon to tell how long the batteries will last but he already stocked up on them so he should be good for a few years.

I have uploaded some pictures of the one attached to the side of the house, the deck and the shed. (I forgot to snap a picture during the day of the shed) but, install was super easy. He bought one first, then ended up buying two more. He loves them! Review 3 Rating: 5/5 Title: Great Product!! Text: [[VIDEOID:5d4001b76b07b4329e94557bda3cb3a8]] Here what i think, i think the motion is great. I hung it up about 15 feet high and it triggers about 25 feet in distance. So far i’ve had one for 1 month and its running strong. I bought another one a day ago.i will update my review.
summary: Reviewers were impressed with the quality of the lights. The batteries lasted about 10 months, thanks to a squirrel and bird who both got on the porch and made nests.
